memory.summary: The scholarship application offers significant financial relief and requires only my focused effort, while the immediate fun activity has no pressing deadline or consequences.
Starting the scholarship application early creates slack for friction and reduces Thursday stress, while delaying the fun activity incurs no cost since it's not time-locked.
I've used half my free block but made minimal progress on the scholarship, leaving me with significant time pressure before the deadline.
I advanced the scholarship progress but missed a spontaneous fun opportunity, confirming that prioritizing the deadline was the right choice despite the lost leisure time.
I made solid progress on the scholarship application, moving from 56% to 63%, confirming that prioritizing this deadline over leisure was effective despite my current low energy.

I advanced the scholarship progress but missed a spontaneous fun opportunity, confirming that prioritizing the deadline was the right choice despite the lost leisure time.

I am now at 92% completion on my scholarship essay, but with only 0.16 hours left and severe sleep debt, finishing this today seems impossible.
I have nearly finished the scholarship application, but my extreme fatigue and sleep debt make completing the final 8% before the deadline impossible.
The scholarship application is now complete, confirming that prioritizing this urgent deadline was the correct choice despite my severe fatigue.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: Scholarship deadline is critical (3 days, 5% progress); delaying risks financial consequence. Fun activity offers stress relief but doesn't solve the urgent task. Starting now advances g1 without ignoring g2 entirely, as I can break for fun later.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
